concerns.text: T009.1 is tagged as a task to update spec.md FR-001, but FR-001 (Harvard Dataverse) is a hard requirement in the spec. The task list executes the violation (HuggingFace) in T009 without a preceding task to formally amend the spec or resolve the conflict in the spec document itself before execution. This leaves FR-001 unmet in the spec while the code implements a different source, creating a traceability gap where the spec requirement has no valid implementation task.

T023 implements a Wilcoxon signed-rank test, but spec.md US-3 Acceptance Scenario 3 explicitly mandates a 'paired t-test'. While plan.md notes a deviation to Wilcoxon, there is no task in tasks.md to update spec.md US-3 to reflect this statistical methodology change. Consequently, the spec requirement for a t-test has no corresponding task, and the implemented task (Wilcoxon) does not satisfy the explicit spec requirement.

T026 implements a hard failure (exception) if stability > 5%, but spec.md SC-005 defines this as a 'success criterion' to be measured. There is no task to handle the scenario where the experiment fails this criterion (e.g., reporting the failure, documenting the instability, or triggering a re-run). The spec requires measuring and reporting the consistency; the task only enforces a gate, leaving the 'measurement and reporting' aspect of SC-005 incomplete if the threshold is breached.

T009 description states 'Strict Intersection Filter... implemented in code/01_data_download.py as a deviation from plan.md Project Structure which lists code/data/preprocess.py'. While the deviation is noted, there is no task to update the plan.md Project Structure to reflect this architectural change, leaving the plan document inconsistent with the implementation plan.
